page: Microsoft Azure E4pds_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the EPDSv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores powered by an Ampere ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor operating at 3.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Additionally, the server is equipped with 154 GB of local SSD storage and is premium storage capable, while lacking any GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities for standard integer and text processing workloads, but show limitations in intensive computational tasks. The Ampere processor achieved a stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1822.68 and a multi-core score of 7252.56, placing both in the average tier. Memory bandwidth for large blocks is also average, with read speeds of 48.18 GB/sec and write speeds of 27.09 GB/sec. However, the system exhibits weak performance in Geekbench multi-core tests and Passmark memory tests. Cryptographic and vector-heavy operations are constrained, as evidenced by a poor Passmark CPU encryption score of 649.7 and the absence of AVX or AMX instruction sets.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E4pds_v5 offers a high memory density relative to its vCPU count on an energy-efficient ARM architecture, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require high-performance x86 vector instructions. It is well-suited for database operations, as demonstrated by an average Passmark database score of 2892.68, as well as text processing and basic web serving. It is less suitable for LLM inference, software compilation, or heavy cryptographic tasks due to poor benchmark results in those categories.
description: Microsoft Azure E4pds_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the EPDSv5 family, featuring 4 dedicated physical cores on an Ampere ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz. It is configured with 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high 8.0 GB per core memory ratio, and includes 154 GB of local storage. Benchmark data reveals average performance in stress-ng CPU tests and large-block memory bandwidth, alongside average results in database operations and text processing. However, it displays weak performance in software compilation, memory latency, and cryptographic tasks, lacking AVX and AMX instruction sets. This memory-optimized profile makes the server suitable for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and basic web serving where ARM architecture compatibility is met, offering a resource-dense configuration without the overhead of high-end compute features.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E4pds_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 cores, 32 GB of RAM, and 154 GB of local storage, optimized for memory-intensive database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E4pds_v5 is an ARM-based virtual machine with 4 dedicated Ampere cores, 32 GB RAM, and 154 GB local storage for memory-intensive tasks.
tagline: An ARM-based virtual machine featuring high memory density and local storage for memory-intensive workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: Powered by a 4-core Ampere ARMv8 Neoverse-N1 processor at 3.0 GHz
Equipped with 32.0 GB RAM providing 8.0 GB per core
Includes 154 GB of local storage with premium storage capability
Delivers average performance in database operations and text processing
Suited for memory-optimized workloads, databases, and basic web serving
